Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Sail card represents the act of harnessing an external, or higher, power to your needs. Just as a ship's sail, or windmill's turbine harnesses the elemental wind to drive the ship or mill, so the card can imply that you will make headway, perhaps travel.

Significator.Plot: The Sail Plot is about being at the mercy of greater powers, and learning to survive these immense happenings. The Sail relies on powerful forces from the Cycle or an Epic that drives the forces of the Plot. The Hooked Characters must exist alongside these powerful forces without being directly threatened, usually. The Sail represents harnessing these forces and in this way, you can create Dragon Riders, Demon-hunters, Elemental Bending Styles, or whatever, depending upon what the forces are.
Significator.Side: A side marked by the Sail should make headway during the events of the plot. The Conflict may drive them across a map to a destination, or through an Ordeal to a Creation. It may even indicate they are also the Embodiment of a larger Plot, and will have a Scene from that Plot, or that this whole Plot is an Act or Tract of a larger Plot.
Significator.Character:
The Sail Significator indicates a Character who has learned to harness some external power for their own ends.

They Gain Yin when they have time to think, rest, or create.

They Gain Yang when they are busy, working or have been given a task.

They Gain Chi when they use external power to accomplish their goal (engines, sails, horses, whatever really), rather than their own personal power.

They also gain +1 Success Levels to attempts to harness external power, and the results.
Significator.Scene: The Sail Scene is usually a leisurely part of the Story. The Characters can usually rest and recover while they travel, although events can turn, external power is not always consistent or constant. Winds can storm as well as becalm. Yarn-Tellers usually play a Sail card to harness some external power to their ends. They might also use it to gain a vehicle or other Descendant, or to power the same. The Sail can also be used to rest and recover somewhat (with a tendency toward Malformed: Amputated Body-part Hitches replacing "Dying").

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Hitches, the bigger the better.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Story Hooks the PCs by getting them all aboard the same vehicle. Often a boat (although trains and even cars will work). Often the other side will also have Embodiments on board the same craft, or that will be encountered at the planned stops or eventual destination.
Yarn.Fray_Description: Travelling can be a Motional Ordeal in its own sense, navigating, surviving, but there is more to this than the journey or the destination. Travel truly can broaden the mind, and learning can be part of the Ordeal, raising the Stakes to Soul or Paradoxical levels.
Yarn.Snag_Description: The Fray is complicated by the situation, sometimes the weather is against you, the wind is blowing you into greater danger, a situation that requires a change of tack. It may be a case of changing the PCs short term goals so they can still accomplish their long term goals.

NPC.Description: The Sailor is always full of stories of far away places they watched glide by their ship. They are steady workers who have the occasional tendency to lose body parts, eyes, or gain Scars, and Exposed Tattoos

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs are headed in the right direction. There's a good wind, and a good chance things are going their way, they might even make a Revelation or make a Gain on the way.
Yarn.History_Description: The Sail indicates that the history that led to the current situation either involved travel, either someone travelled to arrive and created this situation, someone left which created this situation, or someone travelled and brought it back with them.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Sail indicates that the current situation is that of a sail without wind. This can appear as a lack of motile force, lack of fuel, or lack of power.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Sail indicates the solution to the current situation can be solved by travelling, this travel may be to retrieve a specific object from a specific location, but more often the away is more important.

Revelations.About_Description: The information revealed is about the Below Conflict Embodiment. The Below side is lowly, considered beneath the normal scope of the conflict. This might include the Peasants, Plebs, or normal people, depending on the level of the conflict.
Revelations.Info_Description: Information that reveals which Conclusion or option is correct. Like which way the next piece of the puzzle is, or which wire should be cut.
Revelations.Vector_Description: Facts appear that support the evidence, but it isn't directly stated.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is all about past failures. How did the first five test subjects die? Where are the original fixtures? How many people were in that building he designed, you know, the one that fell in the river?
Revelations.Detail_Description: Unreliable details, sometimes the details don't point you in the right direction. They just might not be true, no matter how much you want to believe that the Vampires and Unicorns are working together on some Undead Færy Cavalry, they are natural enemies.

Obstacle.Description: The way ahead is blocked by fog, clouds, smoke or gas. A favourite of villains, who may choose deadly, knock-out, or hallucinogenic chemicals for Traps.

Tarot.Normal: Target may disregard current RT, GRT, Pool Size and take a Full-Action, right now.
Tarot.Crossed: Target is immediately affected by GRT of 2

Ordeal.Stage_Description: The way ahead is fraught with problems, you may be dealing with an unstable floor, or an unstable building, or perhaps the person you are dealing with is themselves unstable.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Keep rowing, I think we're losing them.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: There is some force, beyond the immediate battle field that holds some command upon it. This External force will decide the next Tide of Battle, not the actions of those already fighting. This may include a third force who ride into battle, an attack by a dragon or intervention by law enforcement.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
This is a Fight against a pair of Monsters (for example two Vampires or a Vampire and a Werewolf). Each Monster is at least equal to a PC in power and has 2 ♠ Monster Facets.
  Facet    Monster  
Gossamer ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Wiry — The Monster is immensely physically powerful, although often its form may not reveal it. It often demonstrates supernatural power as well as strength and toughness. A small, strong Goblin, a robot that looks like a little girl, a cybernetically enhanced rat, or even a killer doll (when not an Object Monster) are all examples of a Gossamer Monster. The Wiry Monster can add Gossamer Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever it is underestimated, cared for, or demonstrates its strength. A Wiry monster may add their current Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any action with their strength or toughness. Wiries must spend cards from their Monster Pool as though it was a Prepared Defence to ignore damage.
Inertia ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Unstoppable — The Monster is inexorable, it does not stop and cannot be killed by any normal means. They start play with a Monster Pool of Inertia Score cards. Unstoppable Monsters can add Inertia Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cross a threshold, pass a barrier, or are forced to stop or turn around. Unstoppable Monsters can ignore a number of Wounds equal to the number of cards in their Monster Pool. Wounds stacked beyond the Monster Pool affect them as normal. Inexorable Monsters can also ignore all Hitches with a Boon less than their Monster Pool Size. Whenever an Unstoppable Monster is damaged beyond this limit, it must Discard the Highest Card in the Monster Pool, it may immediately also Discard any Wounds lower than discarded Monster Card, any remaining Wounds are then discarded on a one Monster Pool card per Wound basis. However, while discarding these wounds and cards the Monster must remain still (playing dead) until a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Number of Monster Pool cards Discarded (the initial card is instant, the next discard takes an action, then a second takes a few seconds, the third Wound takes an Ordeal Round, 25 Wounds would take a week). Examples of the Inertial Monster type include the Hungry Dead (Zombies) and most robotic assassins (especially ones from the future).
Key ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Hunter — The Monster is a hunter or tracker, it should favour harrying attacks and hit and run tactics. Typically such a Monster will vanish or flee if it is wounded, only to reappear when healed. The hunter may be fooled by false trails, but never for long. Hunters may add Key Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they spot a track, clue, or similar, that indicates the subject of their hunt. Hunters receive a bonus to their Score or Pips equal to the number of cards in the Monster Pool on any tracking, attacking or hunting actions if they also spend a card from the Monster Pool on the action. Hunters may convert Mortal and Carnage Wounds that they take into Crippling Wounds instead, at a cost of one card from their Monster Pool. In T13, the archetypal version of this Monster is a Bulmäs Pack member from the Wild Hunt, but any Werewolf or Animal Shifter would probably qualify, as would robot cowboys in theme parks that have gone wrong, and any fantasy world’s Elven ranger.
Miasma ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Mutant — The monster is a mutant or chimerical creature made from parts of others. This monster type is the oldest perhaps, you’ll find examples throughout the ancient world, particularly in Greek myth. The Mutant will often be larger, and may be malformed or unsymmetrical, with odd numbers of limbs, eyes, horns, or even heads. The Chimera will blend multiple animals together to create a new creature that combines features of all the “parent” creatures, preserving the best of all species. Mutant Monsters start with Miasma Score Monster Pool. They may add Miasma Draw cards to the Pool every time they accomplish a specified goal, such as eating a human, consuming a soul, bathing in radiation, or just growing a year older, etc. The Monster Pool can be used as a “Mutation” Annex, which can be used to mimic the abilities of the Mutants “parent” creatures, such as a spider’s ability to climb, or an eagle’s ability to fly. The Mutation Annex has a Boon equal to the Value of the Number of Cards in the Monster Pool, and so draws cards equal to the Monster Pool Size Reduced and plays a number of cards equal to the Miasma Draw. The Mutation Annex is often used as a Size Annex for the Mutant. Where a 5 card Monster Pool would create a Boon 16 Annex, and could grant a Kaiju Mutant the size of a Skyscraper. 1950s B-movie monsters are often of this type, giant ants, lizards, spiders, wasps, and even rabbits have been used. Classically of course, Cyclopses, Centaurs, Minotaurs, Satyrs, Gryphons (or Griffins), Hippogriffs, Dragons, Sphinxes, Manticores and of course the Chimera itself are all examples. You can also find examples in superheroes, especially those created by radioactive arachnids, or those with an X-gene.
Trial ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Killer — The Killer Monster is a deadly Combat specialist of some sort. Killers add Trial Draw cards to their Monster Pool each time they kill. Killers automatically add +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to all attacks, and Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) is automatically added to the Pips of any attack as Penetration. Killers must spend Monster Pool cards whenever they will make a Trick either with other cards being played by the Ordeal Pool, or other cards in the Round Pool or a Style Reserve. Killers can be drug-addled assassins, crazed shooters, or just steel-souled soldiers.
Yonder ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Alien — The Monster is an alien, an outsider. It may just be a metaphor for alienation, or literally be an extra-(or ultra)-terrestrial. Such monsters are usually obvious and inhuman, not just to look at, but in the way they think and behave. They may have apparently supernatural powers and abilities, but often make do with advanced technologies (in the form of additional Descendants). They may add Yonder Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they are observed doing their alien / inhuman / weird actions. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any action to do with their alien abilities, technologies, motion or survival (including Dodge, Soak, etc). Alien Monsters must play cards from their Monster Pool to socialise with “non-aliens”. From little grey Eusaiveans, through pod people and pepper-pot shaped engines of hate, all the way to face-hugging space-wasps, there are plenty of aliens.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Example: Spider / Centipede / Wasp / Scorpion / Songbird / Bat
Lea.Notes: Small, often venomous predators, usually insectivores, often leaping, flying, or parachuting

Stress.Description: Stress can interfere with your life, making it more complicated in every way. Self-sabotage can play a part too, stress acts against our better judgement and instincts at times, interfering in what we do.
Stress.Rules: All Actions in the suited Facets (Gossamer, Inertia, Key, Miasma, Trial or Yonder) add a Difficulty equal to the Character’s current Stress in Tao Sway. If a penalised Action Succeeds it will reduce the Character’s Stress by 1.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can make a Character not feel safe in their own home, and many choose to leave. Some will make careful preparations, purchasing a new place, arranging a new job and selling up, others will just abandon their entire life and escape without a plan. Of course, the second types usually end up in a spiralling descent to more Trauma, and worsening situations, but occasionally they will make progress and build a new life for themselves.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma creates a desire to escape their home and situation. The Character may Relieve 1 Stress anytime they make any Action that leads towards escape from their current situation (for example, placing your home on the market, notifiying a landlord you are moving out, applying for a job in another country, being interviewed for a job, or riding a shuttle away will all relieve Stress).

Age.Description: An Age of Dependency is occurring. During this age a society hitches their wagon to another. Sometimes this is a deliberate action by the rulers of the society, to ally with a more powerful society, other times it comes as a result of an invasion or similar geo-political crisis. During the age the society is dependent upon the "root" society for something, often this is a trade agreement, or military self-defence pact with a more powerful partner. Dependence on others can cause friction within a society, especially if this comes after past glories, and the society may feel they are not well enough respected by other allies during this dependence (although often this is simply projection, or rose-tinted nostalgia making some members of the society feel weak compared to their previous position). Typically an age of dependence ends with a breaking away, an indepence revolution, internal collapse, merging into the "root" society, or by politically or technologically manoeuvring into a more dominant position.
Age.Rules: During an Age of Dependency a society is dependent upon another usually larger society, smaller societies may spend the vast majority of their time dependent upon the national or (inter-)planetary society above them. Villages are often associated with towns, towns with counties, counties with states, provinces or principalities, and those with countries, countries with unions and empires etc. Typically a dependent society pays Sway into the root society and the root society returns it as though it were one level higher, but this it is still paid back in the same Sway (e.g. Paying in Chi returns Chi equal to the Value of the Boon).
